CREATE TABLE test_nvarchar (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name NVARCHAR(255));
SELECT * FROM test_nvarchar WHERE name LIKE 'A%';
SELECT DISTINCT name FROM test_nvarchar WHERE LENGTH(name) <= 10;
UPDATE test_nvarchar SET name = 'Updated Name' WHERE id = 1;
CREATE TABLE test_status (status_id INT PRIMARY KEY, status NVARCHAR(50));
SELECT tn.id, tn.name, ts.status FROM test_nvarchar tn JOIN test_status ts ON tn.id = 1 WHERE ts.status = 'Active';
SELECT tn.id, tn.name FROM test_nvarchar tn WHERE tn.name IN (SELECT ts.status FROM test_status ts WHERE ts.status_id = 1);
CREATE TABLE test_logs (log_id INT PRIMARY KEY, log_message NVARCHAR(500));
INSERT INTO test_logs (log_id, log_message) VALUES (1, 'Test log for NVARCHAR boundary scenarios.');
SELECT log_message FROM test_logs WHERE log_id = (SELECT MAX(log_id) FROM test_logs);
SELECT name FROM test_nvarchar WHERE LENGTH(name) BETWEEN 1 AND 255;
INSERT INTO test_nvarchar (id, name) VALUES (4, 'Very Long Name That Exceeds The Buffer Length And Should Fail');
INSERT INTO test_nvarchar (id, name) VALUES (5, REPLACE('This is an nvarchar test.', 'test', 'example'));
